_README: Drives step 6 of the toolkit: backing up important user + application data on Windows (submodules/E_backup_user&application_data.ps1) and restoring it on Linux (Execute on Linux!/submodules/restore_user_and_application_data.sh). 'userProfile' is the STATIC, default-important part of every user's profile directory (C:\Users\<name>). 'applications' is the DYNAMIC, app-specific part: author ONE entry per manifest (B_applications.json) alternative that carries an 'install' object. Inclusions/Exclusions are regex matched against each file's archive-relative path with FORWARD slashes; Exclusions OVERRIDE Inclusions. Keep regex tight so you don't sweep junk in. Keys are matched to staged folders by a shared slug (lowercase; non [a-z0-9-_] stripped), so the same key works on both sides.
_scopes: Each application may declare two independent scopes. 'userdir' = data that lives inside the user profile (e.g. %APPDATA%, %LOCALAPPDATA%) and is governed by 'userdir_safe_to_transfer_to_corresponding_paths_for_linux'. 'system' = data that lives elsewhere because the app is installed machine-wide (e.g. %PROGRAMDATA%, %PROGRAMFILES%) and is governed by 'safe_to_transfer_to_corresponding_paths_for_linux'. This lets some of one app's data land on the corresponding Linux path while the rest goes to the deviated/quarantine directory.
_safe_values: yes = the data works as-is on the corresponding Linux path -> always promote to linuxTarget. 'same version' = promote ONLY when the user answered 'same' to the (4/10) version question; otherwise quarantine. no = never promote (quarantine). 'yes-after-rewrite' = promote, but first apply contentRewrites (always writing a .bak). A rewrite is text-only and reversible; it never applies to binary / database / version-pinned on-disk formats.
_decisionTable: safe==yes -> promote. safe==yes-after-rewrite -> rewrite(+.bak) then promote. safe=='same version' -> promote iff MIGRATE_VERSION_MODE==same else quarantine. safe==no -> quarantine. Quarantine = <restore path>/<key>/<scope>/. Wine apps set wine:true and point linuxTarget into ~/.wine/drive_c so their Windows-format paths keep working under Wine (so they are always 'safe' and are NEVER rewritten).
_contentRewrites: Optional per-scope list. Each item: { files:[regex...], rules:[ {kind:'winpath_to_linux'|'userprofile_to_home'|'drive_map'} | {find:'regex', replace:'text'} ], backup:true }. Named 'kind' transformers are curated + tested; raw find/replace is the author's responsibility. Applies ONLY to plain-text config files. Promotion correctness is never guaranteed by a rewrite alone -- the restore script always tells the user to test the app afterwards.
_excludedByPolicy: Cloud-synced data (OneDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive, iCloud, Box, ...) and ALL browser profile data are NEVER backed up -- they restore from their own service. E_backup_user&application_data.ps1 drops them at collection time, whether online-only or fully downloaded, so do NOT add app entries for browsers or for folders that live under a cloud-sync root.

userProfile._comment: Static important parts of C:\Users\<name>. Each entry: path (relative to the profile), linuxTarget, optional exclude regex list. These always restore verbatim into the target user's home; never rewritten.

PostgreSQL.note: WORKED REWRITE EXAMPLE. Config files (postgresql.conf, pg_hba.conf) are plain text and only differ by path format -> promoted after a winpath_to_linux rewrite. The DATA CLUSTER (PGDATA) is version- AND platform-specific and is NOT file-portable across Windows->Linux even at the same major version; it is excluded here. To migrate the actual databases, run pg_dumpall on Windows and restore with psql on Linux (manual).

Visual Studio Code.note: User settings/keybindings/snippets are portable; extensions reinstall themselves. The workspaceStorage / Cache / logs are excluded as junk.

Claude Code.note: .claude holds prompt history, settings, memory and skills -- all plain JSON/markdown and fully portable to the same path on Linux.

Obsidian.note: Vaults are plain markdown folders; the per-vault .obsidian config is portable. Vault locations are user-defined, so only the app config is auto-captured here -- vault folders travel via the userProfile Documents capture.

KeePass.note: The .kdbx database is a self-contained encrypted file and is fully portable. Config (KeePass.config.xml) may reference Windows plugin paths, so it is quarantined rather than promoted.

Notepad++.note: WINE EXAMPLE. Notepad++ runs under Wine; its config keeps Windows-format paths, so it must land inside the Wine prefix (drive_c) unchanged -- always safe, never rewritten.
